reason_for_decision: The article passes because it frames centralized command as a decision platform problem centered on common operating picture, alert discipline, and interoperability.
strengths: The article is particularly strong on alert flow, role design, and the difference between device aggregation and real command support.
It keeps health visibility, access control, and event logging inside the core platform requirements.
The emphasis on measuring the platform itself makes the guidance more operationally mature.
summary_assessment: This article passes comfortably. It is well structured, practical, and useful for teams designing centralized command platforms that operators can actually trust.
